content.seo_title: Freelancer Guide: Antigravity Template for Collaboration
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with collaboration silos. Learn how to use the Antigravity project template and Claude Code to accelerate your freelance development workflow.
content.answer_block: The 'can't collaborate' bottleneck ends by implementing an Antigravity project template. By standardizing your repository architecture and using Claude Code to sync context, you eliminate configuration drift. This approach forces modularity, enabling multiple agents or developers to work on discrete components without constant merge conflicts or communication overhead.
content.intro:
You’ve been there: the client is waiting, the scope has ballooned, and your codebase has become a monolithic fortress that only you can navigate. Collaboration isn't just failing—it’s non-existent. When you're the only one who can touch the code without breaking the deployment pipeline, you aren't a freelancer; you're a prisoner of your own architecture.

It’s time to move beyond chaotic manual handoffs. The Antigravity project template serves as your architectural blueprint, enforcing strict separation of concerns and automated documentation. Combined with the raw power of Claude Code, you can transform your solo workflow into a scalable, collaborative engine that keeps your clients happy and your delivery velocity at an all-time high.

steps.title: Standardize Repository Architecture
steps.description: Establish a consistent folder structure that separates business logic from UI components, ensuring any collaborator knows exactly where to find specific functionality.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a professional SaaS architecture using the Antigravity template. Focus on a monorepo structure with clear separation between /services, /components, and /types. Ensure all exports are strictly typed.

steps.title: Automate Contextual Documentation
steps.description: Force the codebase to document itself upon every commit, preventing the 'knowledge silo' problem where only the creator understands the logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure an automated documentation hook that parses JSDoc comments and updates the README.md and API.md files whenever a core service file is modified.

steps.title: Implement Modular Service Layers
steps.description: Break the monolith into decoupled services. This allows you to invite other developers to work on specific features without exposing the entire codebase.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor the existing core logic into isolated, interface-driven service modules. Each module must have its own test suite and mock implementation.

steps.title: Configure Collaborative Access Controls
steps.description: Set up strict access boundaries so that remote collaborators can contribute safely without compromising production security.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a .git-hooks and CI/CD configuration that enforces linting and unit tests before any pull request is merged, ensuring only high-quality code enters the main branch.

steps.title: Sync with Claude Code for Real-time Review
steps.description: Use Claude Code to perform automated code reviews on every incoming pull request to maintain architectural integrity.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a custom review agent prompt that checks all new code against the project's architectural guidelines defined in the Antigravity template.

pro_tips: Always define a 'Global Interface' file at the root to ensure all micro-services speak the same data language, reducing integration bugs.
Use ephemeral development environments via Claude Code to verify changes in a clean state before merging to your main branch.
Adopt 'Interface-First Development': write your TypeScript definitions before a single line of business logic is implemented.
Automate your dependency updates weekly to prevent technical debt from accumulating into a collaboration-blocking wall.
Leverage the Antigravity 'hot-reload' feature for service-level testing so you don't have to restart the entire stack for one module change.
resources: Best Practices for SaaS Architecture: https://github.com/patterns/saas
content.next_steps: Once your template is live, invite your first collaborator to push a small feature. Monitor the CI/CD logs to ensure the automated documentation and linting steps trigger correctly.
content.conversion_block: Stop building in isolation and start shipping professional, collaborative systems that your clients will love. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
